headline: Bumiputera procurement quota lacks any performance evaluation
context: The 30% Bumiputera procurement quota for government contracts has never been formally evaluated for effectiveness since its introduction, with no data on whether it achieves its economic restructuring objectives.

cards.big: 30% Bumiputera procurement quota — decades old, never evaluated for actual impact
cards.sub: Government mandates preference but never checked if it works as intended

cards.big: Treasury Instruction 170 mandates Bumiputera set-aside but requires no outcome reporting
cards.sub: Policy perpetuates without evidence of whether it achieves equity objectives

cards.big: RM47 billion in contracts awarded under quota in 2025 alone
cards.sub: Massive public spending allocated by ethnicity without effectiveness assessment

cards.big: Malaysia's UNDP inequality coefficient worsened from 0.399 to 0.411 between 2019-2024
cards.sub: Procurement preferences have not measurably reduced inequality over this period

cards.big: A policy that costs RM47 billion annually but has never been evaluated is faith-based governance
Should the Bumiputera procurement quota be subjected to independent effectiveness review?
